original_hypothesis.title: Manager NAV Bias via maxPriceDeviation Band Enables Systematic Value Extraction
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The manager provides tick prices to computeNAV that are validated to be within maxPriceDeviation of the TWAP. The manager can consistently pick prices at the extreme of the allowed band to systematically inflate NAV during deposit fulfillment (diluting new depositors) or deflate NAV during withdrawal fulfillment (underpaying withdrawers). This creates a persistent extraction opportunity bounded by the deviation band.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even a small per-epoch bias compounds over many fulfillment cycles. The deviation band is a security parameter, but its width directly determines the manager's ability to bias pricing. A wide deviation band (needed for volatile assets) also creates a larger extraction window.
deployment_assumptions: maxPriceDeviation is set to a non-trivial value (e.g., > 100 ticks)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: PanopticVaultAccountant.sol: PoolInfo.maxPriceDeviation is a configurable parameter set via updatePoolsHash. No deployment script to verify actual value.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Math.abs(managerPrice - TWAP) <= maxPriceDeviation allows manager to choose prices at band extremes for each pool and conversion oracle

preconditions: maxPriceDeviation is wide enough to materially affect NAV
Vault has significant positions requiring tick-based valuation

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Manager chooses poolPrice, token0Price, token1Price within deviation band
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Systematic band-edge pricing across all 3 price inputs per pool
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Per-epoch extraction up to deviation_band_width × position_notional × number_of_price_inputs

economic_questions: What are typical maxPriceDeviation values in production?
How does compounding across multiple pools amplify the bias?
What is the annualized extraction rate for realistic parameters?
validation_requirements: Calculate NAV at band extremes vs center for typical position portfolios
Model cumulative extraction over 100+ fulfillment cycles
Compare extraction magnitude to typical vault returns

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 0.1-1% per fulfillment cycle depending on deviation band width

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Depositors or withdrawers depending on bias direction
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Proportional to deviation band width and position sizes
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Cumulative extraction across all fulfillment cycles

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Realistic maxPriceDeviation values and position sizes
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: NAV sensitivity analysis to price deviations

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
